All rock chip assay results, including assays of historical sampling from the WAMEX dada base, are located in Appendix 2.
 
    Alaska (M21/086, M21/125).
 
The Alaska area (Figure 1) consists of multiple historical workings and mines within a relatively small area. Historical sampling of these
workings is illustrated in Figure 10. Rock chip sampling by Fairbairn Goldfields is included in Appendix 2.
 
Recorded production, listed below, is derived from near surface gold bearing quartz veins.
Klondyke: 191 tons @ 12 g/t Au for 74 ozs of gold.
Moonlight (adjacent to Alaska): 2135 tons @ 30.5 g/t Au for 2130 ozs of gold.
Klondyke West: 46 tons @ 8 g/t Au. 
Ada Mary & Ivy May: 67 tons @ 16.1 g/t Au for 34 ozs gold.
 
The Alaska - Alaska West area in Figure 11 is covered by colluvium and some alluvium with sparse outcrop (shaded area in Figure 11).
The area is prospective for gold mineralisation, with the 370m gap between the 2 prospects remaining unexplored. If mineralisation is
confirmed between the two prospects, a potential open pit resource of 60K – 80K tonnes may exist.
